item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah the idea is fun but there are quite a few inconsistencies as pointed out in other posts. The largest one for me is the sterilisation of the scips and the killing of the only female instance. Surely one of the biggest threats they pose is if they can cross breed with non-anomalous rabbits and there are still some loose? -1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This doesn't do too much for me. The concept of "reverse were-rabbits" sounds like it has a lot of potential, but this article just says "they turn into humans and they hunt rabbits" and that's it. I like that there are multiple different types of hunters, but I'd like to see more expansion on the history of reverse were-rabbits, how they behave, local histories. No-vote, sadly

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Details in the table contradict the description.

Such as hunter-forms discussing wabbit hunting in specifically American English; but -1 and -2 are particularly noted as Polish-speaking, -3 is pre-verbal, and it would be noteworthy if -5 didn't use the Queen's English.

Also claws morph into firearms; but -3 and -5 don't use guns. It's possible they "grow" guns that they then don't use; but any good researcher would note that.

Last but not least, all the bunnykin were rendered infertile before the Foundation caught them. So what offspring is -5 supposed to be targeting?

The description would need a rather major re-phrasing to make it consistent with recorded observations.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You, since these are Reverse Were-Rabbits, you could have made it so that they transform into humans on new moons.

+1, for taking a good spin on a cliché.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yep.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fur and claws will be changed to form hunting garb and gunpowder weapons.

SCP-1282-3 Humanoid with an appearance similar to a Neanderthal. Does not speak beyond growling and grunting. Clothed in what appears to be a rabbit pelt. Hunts indiscriminately.

SCP-1282-5 Female humanoid, identifies by the name "Vale Delon." Dresses in traditional British royalty hunting garb, circa 1879. Only instance which will refuse firearms, preferring to terminate rabbits with blunt trauma.

…err, the Neanderthal uses firearms? Now I'm scared.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Also noting because I just remembered, I got the original idea for this in far recon ages ago. Minmin came up with it, and told me I could write it. Several months later, I did.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, instead of testing if they can reproduce and if the offspring carries the anomaly, they go and kill the (apparently) only female?
Not exactly smart.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, you'd think they would step in once one instance starts attacking the other.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I expected to see Elmer Fudd on the list of identities…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Numbers 1 & 2 speak Polish in addition to American English, or instead of American English? Also, are these Night of the Lepus were-humans longer-lived than regular rabbits? Do/can they "infect" non-anomalous rabbits?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not bad, not bad at all. A little extended, what with the gunpowder weapons and everything, but not bad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like they should be harder to kill than regular rabbits. To fit the mythos better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eh, it's German. I'm not surprised to see it paired with a Polish-speaking identity.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How else do you find out if they can recognize other infected rabbits as being like them?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eh. I don't care about this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My only problem is that Alan Rupp doesn't seem to sound like a polish name at all. Other than that, have my upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No, see, I understand what it's getting at, but that on its own is not enough to interest me. It's not enough to simply invert a trope, in my book. Usually, it needs more done with it, which this doesn't.

Musing more about it, I'm meh enough on this to downvote it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I believe the idea is that rabbits would be afraid of rabbit hunters, in the sense that they can understand rabbit hunting. The reverse were-rabbits target rabbits in the same way were-wolves traditionally seem to hunt out humans. I liked it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, homicidal wererabbits? What the hell?

I'm kind of surprised that they would allow one instance to kill another, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a novel concept, but… it doesn't do anything exceptionally interesting with that concept. WHY, exactly, is there such a thing as a reverse were-rabbit? Along with other questions that I could ask that you could answer.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm rather meh on this one.
That said, "offspring" , not "children" - children are human offspring. "Social associates" also feels exceedingly weird used on non-anomalous rabbits

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's actually a continuation of the above error, I was trying to have the one which focused on the mating partners do the killing but then numbers are hard

e: and fixed

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1282-1 Claims to be a 34 year old humanoid male

SCP-1282-4 Claims to be a humanoid male named "Roadkill"

SCP-1282-1 and SCP-1282-4 were allowed to become mating partners

There is some kinky stuff going on in the Foundation.

e: I understand the part where -1 kills -4 for not recognizing that it's a were-rabbit as well, but why the mating partner part? Unless this SCP is about gay rabbits I don't really understand the test. I like the SCP itself though, especially the remorse part.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A mistake-I will feex.

e: fix'd :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My only real nitpik is you said the test was between 1282-1 and 1282-5, when 1282-4 was supposedly the dead one. I like it though. +1
